i-dí-ni-a-ti-ma IGI GIR
sa A-ur í-bu-tí-ni
ni-di-in
This deposition records a disagreement between Idi-Suen and Mannu-
ki-Aur about a slave which belonged to a certain Rabi-Aur, but whom
Mannu-ki-Aur had seized because he would have brought him.
Ma-nu-ki-A-ùr
IR sa GAL-A-ur
is-ba-at-ma um-ma
I-dí-Sú-en6-ma IR
wa-i-ra-am um-ma
Ma-nu-ki-A-ùr-ma
IR á-a-am ú-láú-a-ra-kum
um-ma I-dí-Sú-en6-ma
DUMU I-ba-a-ma IR
wa-í-ra-am-ma
ma-sa-ku a-na-ku
IRú-ta-ra-kum
su-ma IR lá áp-qi-id-ku-um
KU.BABBAR ma-lá ta-á-qú-lu
a-a-qá-lá-kum a-na a-wa-tim
a-ni-a-tim kà-ru-um
Hu-ra-ma i-dí-ni-a-tí-ma
IGI su-ga-ri-a-e a A-ur
sí-bu-tí-ni ni-dí-in
